title: Order fulfillment saga example with compensations
task: Create a complete order fulfillment saga example at examples/order-saga/ that demonstrates durable workflow compensation patterns. The workflow processes an order through: charge payment, reserve inventory, ship order, confirm. If any step fails after earlier steps succeeded, the workflow compensates by reversing completed steps (refund payment, release inventory). This is the canonical durable workflow showcase. Follow the exact structure of the existing examples/hello-world/ example on main.

requirements.description: Create a Gleam workflow at examples/order-saga/src/order_saga.gleam using aion_flow. The workflow accepts OrderInput (order_id, item, quantity, amount), dispatches 4 sequential activities (charge_payment, reserve_inventory, ship_order, confirm_order). If ship_order fails, dispatch compensating activities release_inventory and refund_payment in reverse order. Return Ok(OrderConfirmation) on success or Error(SagaFailed) with compensation details on failure.
requirements.acceptance: gleam build succeeds and the workflow correctly sequences forward steps and compensations

requirements.description: Create a packager at examples/order-saga/packager/ following the hello-world packager pattern. Build order-saga.aion with manifest declaring all 6 activities.

Create examples/order-saga/worker.py implementing all 6 activity handlers. ship_order checks SIMULATE_SHIPPING_FAILURE env var to allow triggering the compensation path. Activities should log what they're doing so the demo is visual.
requirements.acceptance: Worker serves all activities. With SIMULATE_SHIPPING_FAILURE=true the saga compensates.

requirements.description: Create examples/order-saga/README.md documenting: saga pattern explanation, build steps, happy path demo, compensation path demo, what to observe in the event history.
requirements.acceptance: A developer can follow the README and run both paths

context: Follow examples/hello-world/ structure exactly (gleam.toml with aion_flow path dep, packager/Cargo.toml with aion-package dep, worker.py with aion-worker SDK, copy-pasteable README). The aion_flow API: workflow.define() for entry contract, workflow.run() for activity dispatch. Update dev-config.toml workflow_packages array to include examples/order-saga/order-saga.aion when preloading the example.
